mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows despite low rates.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by 2 percentage points, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion regimes, or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth falls below 2.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside falling unemployment indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive power when PAYEMS growth falls below 100000 per month, or the interaction term shows negative coefficients in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and reduced construction sector demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal becomes positive when FEDFUNDS drops below 0.5 percent.
